research_sources.verification_notes: Confirmed Boots on the Ground's 24/7 peer support helpline number (1-833-677-2668) on the official website.
Verified the Farmer Wellness Initiative’s toll-free number (1-866-267-6255) and 24/7 availability for Ontario farmers on the official site.
Confirmed Fem’aide’s phone/SMS contact (1-877-336-2433) and 24/7 French-language support for women experiencing violence, via the official Fem’aide website.
Found that LGBT YouthLine offers support through text (647-694-4275), email, and online chat for youth 16-29, and confirmed service hours (Sun–Fri 4:00pm–9:30pm) via a 211 Ontario listing.
Verified that ONTX (Ontario Online & Text Crisis) is accessed by texting “SUPPORT” to 258258 and is available daily from 2pm to 2am, according to Distress and Crisis Ontario.
Confirmed the Jordan’s Principle Call Centre number (1-855-572-4453) and its 24/7 availability on the Indigenous Services Canada official page.
Verified Naseeha Mental Health’s 24/7 toll-free helpline (1-866-627-3342) for call or text support, via official information (world’s first 24/7 Muslim peer support helpline).
Confirmed BounceBack Ontario’s contact number (1-866-345-0224) and eligibility (Ontario residents 15+ with OHIP; free program) through official program documentation.
Verified Parkinson Canada’s helpline number (1-888-664-1974), support email, and hours of operation (Mon–Fri 9am–4pm ET) on the official contact page.
Checked Ontario Health’s resource confirming the Talk Tobacco quit line (1-833-998-8255) for First Nations, Inuit, and Métis clients, and noted it is free and available in multiple languages.
Confirmed the Ontario Legal Information Centre’s toll-free line (1-844-343-7462) offers a free 30-minute lawyer consultation to all Ontario residents, from CLEO’s official information.
